creator: ports@grosbein.net
text:
31.05.2012 02:30, Mikolaj Golub ÐÉÛÅÔ:
> Eugene, thanks for the report!
> 
> Looking at the code now I am not sure that the error flag is set properly in
> all cases. I don't recall my reasoning when I was implementing it but the last
> condition (prp->min == 0 && prp->max == 0 && prp->count < 0) looks wrong for
> me now. It means "set error if we failed to count processes due to a system
> error and both min and max are zero".
> 
> Looking at the net-snmp code, it treats the condition "both min and max are
> zero" as "no processes matching this name should be run". I think bsnmp-ucd
> should follow this semantic.
> 
> Please see the attached patch. Does it make sense for you?
> 

Yes, it's fine then.

While you are here, wouldn't it be good to indicate
"Failed to count processes" case with prErrMessage too?

--- mibpr.c.orig        2012-05-31 12:53:03.000000000 +0700
+++ mibpr.c     2012-05-31 12:54:18.000000000 +0700
@@ -367,7 +367,9 @@ op_prTable(struct snmp_context * context
 
                case LEAF_prErrMessage:
                        if (prp->count < 0) {
-                               buf[0] = '\0'; /* Failed to count processes. */
+                               snprintf((char*)buf, sizeof(buf),
+                                   "Failed to count %s processes.",
+                                   prp->names);
                        } else if (prp->min && prp->count < prp->min) {
                                snprintf((char*)buf, sizeof(buf),
                                    "Too few %s running (# = %d)",

Another thing that bothers me: bsnmp_ucd seems to check last component
of process pathname only. For multiuser machines that seems unsafe.
